However, all the DateTimeZoneCutover tests are still failing or unchanged. These tests likely rely on proper handling of timezone transitions and daylight saving changes. If the provider isn't correctly being set to UTCProvider when ZoneInfoProvider fails, it could cause issues in these tests.

But wait, in the mutant code, line 187 checks if provider is null and sets it to UTCProvider. So when control exits the try-catch block, provider remains null because creating ZoneInfoProvider failed.

Then, line 186 checks if provider is null and sets it to UTCProvider. That should work correctly because if ZoneInfoProvider creation fails, provider will be null, so it falls back to UTCProvider.

So why are some tests still failing? Maybe the issue is that when ZoneInfoProvider isn't available, the code defaults to UTCProvider, which might not handle certain timezones or transitions correctly, leading to test failures. Alternatively, perhaps the mutation didn't affect these tests because they were expecting a different behavior.

But the test that changed from failing to passing (TestMonthDay_Properties) isn't adjacent in terms of code location. So maybe the mutation didn't directly affect the lines where this test's failure was located.
